verses.text: Asaph begs God not to keep silent while enemies craftily plot to cut off His people from being a nation. The threat is to Israel's very existence. When God's people are imperilled, the church cries for Him to act.

Asaph begs God not to keep silent while enemies craftily plot to cut off His people from being a nation. The threat is to Israel's very existence. When God's people are imperilled, the church cries for Him to act.
Many nations have joined in one confederacy, allied 'against thee.' Their league is ultimately against God. To war on God's people is to war on God Himself, who will not be neutral.

He asks God to deal with them as with Midian and Sisera of old, who schemed to seize 'the houses of God.' He pleads past judgments as precedent. The God who scattered ancient foes can scatter these.

Let them be like whirling chaff and stubble before the storm and fire, swept away. He prays for their overthrow. The enemies of God, however massed, are as light as dust before Him.

Yet his deepest aim is that, shamed, 'men may seek thy name' and know that God 'art the most high over all the earth.' Even judgment serves mercy's end. He longs that defeated foes may come to know the true God.
